pi_items.source: setup_task.sh:29 - hardcoded USERS array. One is randomly selected as SUSPECT at setup time
install_nx_witness.sh:32, NetworkOptix official documentation confirms Nx Witness has audit trail functionality

pi_items.verified_value: Identify which user accessed the 'Secure Lab Camera' from audit logs, write username to /home/ga/leak_report.txt

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic security investigation task using Nx Witness VMS. The task involves identifying a randomly-selected suspect from five hardcoded users (john.smith, sarah.connor, rick.deckard, ellen.ripley, james.bond) who accessed the 'Secure Lab Camera'. The ground truth suspect username is stored in /root/ground_truth_suspect.txt (root-only read access). The agent must navigate the Nx Witness audit trail interface and identify the correct username, writing it to /home/ga/leak_report.txt. Both file paths specified in metadata are correct and match usage throughout setup_task.sh and export_result.sh.
